title: Secure-by-design development lifecycle
description: Products with digital elements shall be designed, developed, and produced in accordance with essential cybersecurity requirements, ensuring security is integrated from the earliest stages of the product lifecycle.
evidenceGuidance: Documented secure development lifecycle (SDLC) procedures, threat modelling records, design review minutes, security requirements traceability matrix.
applicabilityNotes: For space systems: applies to on-board software, firmware, ground segment applications, and mission planning tools. Covers the full chain from development to deployment on the satellite bus.

title: Default secure configuration
description: Products shall be delivered with secure default configurations, including the ability to reset to the original secure state, and shall not ship with known exploitable vulnerabilities.
evidenceGuidance: Default configuration documentation, hardening guides, baseline security configuration audits, evidence of configuration review before deployment.
applicabilityNotes: Satellite firmware and ground station software must ship with minimal attack surface. Default telecommand authentication must be enabled, not optional.

title: Vulnerability handling process
description: Manufacturers shall identify and document vulnerabilities and components contained in the product, maintain a software bill of materials (SBOM), and apply effective and regular tests and reviews.
evidenceGuidance: SBOM in CycloneDX or SPDX format, vulnerability scanning reports, dependency analysis results, penetration test reports.
applicabilityNotes: Critical for space systems using COTS components. SBOMs must cover both ground segment libraries and on-board software dependencies including RTOS modules.

title: Coordinated vulnerability disclosure
description: Manufacturers shall establish a coordinated vulnerability disclosure policy, provide a contact address for vulnerability reporting, and process reported vulnerabilities without delay.
evidenceGuidance: Published vulnerability disclosure policy, security.txt file, VDP contact records, evidence of timely triage and response to reported vulnerabilities.
applicabilityNotes: Space operators should establish sector-specific disclosure channels. Coordinate with ENISA and national CSIRTs for vulnerabilities affecting critical space infrastructure.

title: Vulnerability remediation through security updates
description: Address and remediate vulnerabilities without delay, including by providing security updates. Where a security patch is not possible, provide mitigation guidance to users.
evidenceGuidance: Patch management records, security advisory publications, mean time to remediate metrics, mitigation guidance documents for unpatched vulnerabilities.
applicabilityNotes: On-orbit assets require special consideration: firmware updates must be validated in ground-based simulators before uplink. Maintain rollback capability for all patches.

title: Security update delivery mechanism
description: Ensure security updates are made available to users free of charge and in a timely manner, with advisory information including potential impact and remediation guidance.
evidenceGuidance: Update distribution mechanism documentation, security advisory templates, evidence of free update availability, user notification records.
applicabilityNotes: For satellite operators: secure OTA update channels for on-board software. Ground segment updates must use authenticated delivery with integrity verification.

title: Support period and end-of-life planning
description: Manufacturers shall ensure vulnerability handling and security updates for a minimum support period proportionate to the expected product lifetime.
evidenceGuidance: Documented support period per product, end-of-life transition plan, evidence of continued security monitoring during support period.
applicabilityNotes: Satellite missions often span 15+ years. Security update support must cover the full mission lifetime. Plan for long-term cryptographic agility as algorithms may be deprecated.

title: Confidentiality of stored and transmitted data
description: Products shall protect the confidentiality of data, including at rest and in transit, using state-of-the-art encryption or other technical means.
evidenceGuidance: Encryption implementation documentation, key management procedures, TLS/DTLS configuration records, data classification and handling policy.
applicabilityNotes: Applies to TT&C links, inter-satellite links, and ground network segments. Use space-qualified encryption for uplink/downlink. Consider quantum-resistant algorithms for long-duration missions.

title: Integrity of stored and processed data
description: Products shall protect the integrity of stored, transmitted, and processed data, commands, and configuration against unauthorized manipulation.
evidenceGuidance: Integrity verification mechanisms documentation, digital signature implementation, tamper detection records, command authentication procedures.
applicabilityNotes: Critical for telecommand authentication. All commands to spacecraft must be integrity-protected. Telemetry data must include integrity checks to detect tampering during downlink.

title: Access control and authentication
description: Products shall implement appropriate access control mechanisms, including authentication, identity management, and minimal privilege principles.
evidenceGuidance: Access control policy, role-based access matrix, authentication mechanism documentation, privileged access management records, MFA deployment evidence.
applicabilityNotes: Ground station access must enforce strong authentication. Mission control systems require role-based access with operator/supervisor separation. Consider hardware tokens for critical telecommand authorization.

title: Logging and monitoring of security events
description: Products shall provide logging capabilities for security-relevant events, including access attempts, configuration changes, and anomalies.
evidenceGuidance: Logging policy, log retention configuration, SIEM integration evidence, sample security event logs, monitoring dashboard screenshots.
applicabilityNotes: Space systems must log all telecommand transmissions, ground station access events, and telemetry anomalies. Ensure log integrity through cryptographic chaining or write-once storage.

title: Minimisation of attack surface
description: Products shall minimise the attack surface, including external interfaces. Unused ports, protocols, and services shall be disabled.
evidenceGuidance: Attack surface assessment report, network diagram with exposed interfaces, port scan results, disabled services documentation, interface hardening records.
applicabilityNotes: Satellite bus systems should disable unused CCSDS service types. Ground stations must minimize internet-facing services. Restrict debug and diagnostic interfaces in production firmware.

title: Technical documentation
description: Manufacturers shall draw up technical documentation containing all relevant information to demonstrate conformity with essential cybersecurity requirements.
evidenceGuidance: Technical documentation package including architecture diagrams, threat model, security testing results, SBOM, configuration guides, and conformity assessment records.
applicabilityNotes: For space products: include spacecraft bus security architecture, link budget security analysis, ground segment network security documentation, and FMECA covering cyber scenarios.

title: User security information
description: Manufacturers shall provide users with clear and understandable instructions for secure installation, operation, and disposal of the product.
evidenceGuidance: User-facing security documentation, secure deployment guide, decommissioning procedures, residual risk disclosure.
applicabilityNotes: Satellite operators must receive clear guidance on secure ground station deployment, key ceremony procedures, and end-of-mission data sanitization for deorbiting assets.

title: Incident reporting to ENISA
description: Manufacturers shall notify ENISA of any actively exploited vulnerability and any severe incident with impact on product security within 24 hours of becoming aware.
evidenceGuidance: Incident reporting procedures, ENISA notification templates, evidence of timely submissions, designated reporting contact, records of 24-hour compliance.
applicabilityNotes: Space operators must be prepared for dual reporting: CRA notifications to ENISA and NIS2 notifications to national CSIRTs. Establish clear internal escalation paths for zero-day exploitation of space system components.

title: User notification of incidents
description: Manufacturers shall inform users of the product about the incident and, where applicable, about corrective measures that the user can deploy to mitigate the impact.
evidenceGuidance: User notification procedures, security advisory templates, evidence of user communication, corrective action guidance documents.
applicabilityNotes: Downstream satellite service customers must be promptly informed of security incidents affecting data integrity or availability. Include impact assessment for dependent services.

title: Conformity assessment for critical products
description: Critical products with digital elements shall undergo conformity assessment by a notified body or through an approved European cybersecurity certification scheme.
evidenceGuidance: Conformity assessment report, notified body certificate, EU declaration of conformity, audit findings and corrective actions, CE marking documentation.
applicabilityNotes: Space system components classified as critical (e.g., satellite command receivers, encryption modules, ground station authentication systems) may require third-party assessment under CRA Class II.

title: Internal conformity assessment and self-declaration
description: For non-critical products, manufacturers may perform internal conformity assessment (Module A) and issue an EU declaration of conformity.
evidenceGuidance: Internal assessment procedures, self-assessment checklist, EU declaration of conformity document, technical file completeness review.
applicabilityNotes: Non-critical ground segment software tools and monitoring dashboards may qualify for self-assessment. Maintain thorough documentation as market surveillance authorities may audit.
